accommodation-modal.update-integration-p:
Reducing the sync period stops sync beyond that period. Manage that period on the channel.
(If the channel limits the sales period, its policy applies.)

Extending the sync period may expose packages without required settings. Set carefully per your policy.
(If the channel limits the sales period, its policy applies.)

You can set the sync period from 30 to 180 days, including yesterday and today.
Inventory and rates for the selected period will be synced with the channel.
(If the channel limits the sales period, its policy takes precedence.)

subscription-modal.content.terms:
Subscription & Refund Policy
┬╖ Up to 10 rooms: Base rate applies, not per-room. 
<1>┬╖ </1>Availability only: Base 50,000 KRW (VAT excl.) 
<1>┬╖ </1>Availability & Rate: Base 100,000 KRW (VAT excl.) 
┬╖ Monthly subs are non-refundable; service continues until next billing. 
┬╖ Immediate annual cancel refunds remaining period to original payment method. 
┬╖ Canceled subscriptions can be resubscribed anytime. 
┬╖ Cancel annual sub within 7 days: Refund excludes usage days at 'discounted price'. 
┬╖ Cancel annual sub after 7 days: Refund excludes usage days at 'regular price'. 
┬╖ Terminated subs cannot be restored; resubscribe to use again. 
┬╖ Payment failure grants 1-day grace. (Service stops if unpaid within grace; check status.) 
┬╖ Once failed payment succeeds, subscription reactivates immediately.

subscription-prompt.modal.section.requir: 50,000 KRW for up to 10 rooms, 5,000 KRW per additional room.
subscription-prompt.modal.terms:
Subscription Information & Refund Policy
тАвSubscription fees include VAT and services can be used until the next billing date upon cancellation.
тАвCanceled subscriptions can be renewed at any time.
тАвFor immediate cancellation, the remaining subscription period will be refunded to the original payment method.
тАвIf canceled more than 7 days after the billing date, 10% of the subscription fee will be deducted as a penalty, and 90% will be refunded.
тАвCanceled subscriptions cannot be reactivated. To resume the service, a new subscription must be purchased.
тАвIf a recurring payment fails or remains unpaid, a 1-day grace period will be provided. (Ensure payment is completed within the grace period to avoid service interruption.)
тАвIf a failed subscription payment is successfully processed later, the subscription will resume from the payment date.

terms.private:
Vendit Co., Ltd. (тАЬthe CompanyтАЭ) complies with the Personal Information Protection Act and other related laws to protect the personal information of representatives and businesses managing accommodations (тАЬCustomersтАЭ). This Privacy Policy applies to the Vendit Channel Management System (тАЬVCMSтАЭ or тАЬthe SolutionтАЭ), including its online, mobile, and application-based services, and outlines how the Company collects, uses, and safeguards personal information.

1. Purpose of Processing Personal Information and Items Collected
The Company processes personal information for the following purposes and collects the following items:

Purpose of Processing
тАв Providing, maintaining, and managing the Solution.
тАв Membership registration and management.

Personal Information Collected
тАв Mandatory Information:
тАв User ID, password
тАв Business registration number, business name, address
тАв Business type and category
тАв Contact personтАЩs name, email, and phone number
тАв Digital certificate ID and password
тАв OTA registration details

2. Provision of Personal Information to Third Parties
The Company does not disclose personal information to third parties without prior consent unless:
тАв It aligns with the original purpose of collection or customary business practices.
тАв Necessary safety measures, such as pseudonymization or encryption, are in place.

3. Retention and Usage Period of Personal Information
The Company retains personal information for the following periods:
тАв Standard Retention: Up to five years after membership termination.
тАв Legal Requirements: Certain data may be retained longer, as mandated by law.
тАв Login records: 3 months
тАв Communication records: 12 months

4. Destruction of Personal Information
Personal information is destroyed promptly after the retention period expires or the purpose of processing is achieved.

Destruction Procedure
тАв Data is identified and approved for deletion by the Data Protection Officer.

Destruction Methods
тАв Printed data: Shredded or incinerated.
тАв Electronic data: Irrevocably deleted using secure technical methods.

5. Rights of Customers and How to Exercise Them
Customers have the right to:
тАв Access, correct, delete, or restrict processing of their personal information.
тАв Withdraw consent or terminate membership.
Requests can be submitted via email, phone, or written communication to the Data Protection Officer. The Company will promptly address these requests.

6. Use of Automated Information Collection Devices (Cookies)
The Company uses cookies to enhance user experience and optimize service delivery.

Purpose of Cookies
тАв Ensure secure and efficient service access.
тАв Provide personalized experiences.

Managing Cookies
тАв Users can adjust browser settings to refuse or delete cookies. However, this may limit certain functionalities.

7. Technical and Administrative Safeguards
To ensure the security of personal information, the Company implements:

Technical Measures
тАв Regular data backups and antivirus software.
тАв Encryption for secure data transmission.
тАв Firewalls and access control to prevent unauthorized access.

Administrative Measures
тАв Staff training on data protection policies.
тАв Limiting access to authorized personnel only.

8. Data Protection Officer
The Data Protection Officer oversees compliance with data protection laws and addresses customer inquiries.
тАв Name: Eungyu Lee
тАв Position: CTO, Development Team
тАв Email: eungyu@vendit.co.kr
тАв Phone: +82-1644-5442

9. Personal Information Inquiries and Complaints
For concerns related to the handling of personal information or potential breaches, Customers may contact the Data Protection Officer. The Company will cooperate with investigations and strive for prompt resolution.

10. Changes to the Privacy Policy
This Privacy Policy is effective as of June 1, 2023.
Any updates, additions, or modifications will be announced in advance via the тАЬNoticesтАЭ section of the CompanyтАЩs website.
terms.service:
Article 1 (Purpose)
These Terms and Conditions define the rights, obligations, and responsibilities of Vendit Co., Ltd. (тАЬthe CompanyтАЭ) and users (тАЬUsersтАЭ) regarding the use of the Vendit Channel Management System (тАЬthe SolutionтАЭ), which includes online, mobile, and application-based services.

Article 2 (Definitions)
1. Solution: A system provided by the Company that supports functions such as member management, room sales record management, and payment program integration.
2. Service: Room sales record management services provided through the Solution.
3. Room Sales Record Management: Automated login to online travel agencies (OTAs) using stored credentials to manage and review room sales records collectively.
4. Member: A person or entity registered with the Solution who continuously uses the services provided by the Company.
5. User: A Member accessing the Solution under these Terms and Conditions.
6. Service Fees: Fees charged for using the SolutionтАЩs basic and additional services.

Article 3 (Notice, Explanation, and Amendment of Terms)
1. The Company will disclose these Terms and Conditions, along with necessary business and contact information, on the SolutionтАЩs homepage for easy access.
2. The Company may amend these Terms in compliance with applicable laws.
3. Amendments will be announced at least seven (7) days prior to their effective date. For changes unfavorable to Users, a notice period of thirty (30) days will be provided.

Article 4 (Formation of the Agreement)
1. The Agreement is established when a User consents to these Terms, applies for the Solution, and the Company approves the application.
2. Users must provide accurate information during registration, and the Company may verify their identity.
3. The Company reserves the right to withhold or reject applications under specific conditions, such as false information or unauthorized use.

Article 5 (Provision of Services)
1. The Company provides services, including automated login to OTAs, room sales record management, and related functions.
2. Services may be modified due to legal, business, or technical changes, with Users promptly notified of significant modifications.

Article 6 (Service Interruption)
1. Services may be temporarily suspended for maintenance, technical issues, or emergencies.
2. Users will be compensated for damages caused by interruptions unless the Company proves the absence of negligence.

Article 7 (Membership Registration)
1. Membership is granted when Users register and agree to these Terms.
2. The Company may deny membership for reasons such as false registration or failure to provide required documents.

Article 8 (User Obligations)
1. Users must provide accurate information and comply with these Terms and applicable laws.
2. Users must secure their account credentials and report any unauthorized access immediately.

Article 9 (Service Fees)
1. Users are required to pay fees as determined by the Company.
2. Additional charges may apply for optional features.

Article 10 (Payment Methods)
1. Service fees are primarily paid via credit card.
2. Payments may be recurring monthly, one-time for additional features, or annual.

Article 11 (Exclusion of Consumer Protection Laws)
These Terms govern business-to-business transactions, and consumer protection laws do not apply.

Article 12 (Use and Termination of the Solution)
1. The Solution may be used immediately after the Agreement is established.
2. Users may terminate use at any time, but all outstanding fees must be settled.

Article 13 (Membership Withdrawal and Disqualification)
1. Members may withdraw at any time by notifying the Company.
2. The Company may limit, suspend, or revoke membership for violations of these Terms.

Article 14 (Notices to Users)
1. Notices will be sent to the UserтАЩs registered contact details.
2. General notices may be posted on the SolutionтАЩs homepage for at least one week.

Article 15 (Modification and Cancellation of Agreements)
1. Users may request modifications or cancellations of agreements, subject to applicable laws.
2. The Company is not liable for modifications unless proven to result from negligence.

Article 16 (Fulfillment of Agreements)
1. Users must fulfill their contractual obligations as specified in the Agreement.
2. The Company may request information about contract execution for advertising or promotional purposes.

Article 17 (Information Protection)
1. Personal information will be handled per the Privacy Policy.
2. Confidential information must be explicitly identified by the User when provided to the Company.

Article 18 (Management of User ID and Password)
1. Users are responsible for managing their IDs and passwords.
2. Unauthorized use must be reported to the Company immediately.

Article 19 (Termination of Agreements)
1. Either party may terminate the Agreement for breaches or by mutual consent.
2. Penalties apply for contract violations based on the remaining term.

Article 20 (Compensation for Damages)
Users are liable for damages caused by breaches of these Terms.

Article 21 (Limitation of Liability)
1. The Company is not liable for damages caused by User negligence or third-party services.
2. Force majeure events exempt the Company from liability.

Article 22 (Intellectual Property Rights)
1. The Company retains all rights to the Solution.
2. Users may not modify, distribute, or commercialize the Solution without prior consent.

Article 23 (Confidentiality)
Both parties must keep proprietary information confidential during and after the Agreement.

Article 24 (Prohibition of Transfer)
Rights and obligations under the Agreement may not be transferred without prior written consent.

Article 25 (Dispute Resolution)
1. Disputes will be resolved amicably wherever possible.
2. Complaints will be prioritized and addressed promptly.

Article 26 (Governing Law)
1. These Terms are governed by the laws of the Republic of Korea.
2. Disputes will be resolved in the competent court under Korean law.

Article 27 (Miscellaneous Provisions)
Any matters not covered in these Terms will be governed by relevant laws and practices.


Addendum (Effective Date)
These Terms and Conditions take effect as of June 1, 2023.
